Event: Oversees occupation and exploitation of Bajor as Prefect
Explanation: He gains rank and power for decades while Bajorans suffer mass death and exploitation.

Event: Has Cardassia join the Dominion, putting his people in harm's way
Explanation: He gains short-term power but triggers catastrophic war and genocide against Cardassians.

Event: Keeps Kira’s mother as a mistress/sex slave
Explanation: He indulges his desires and ego while coercively exploiting and imprisoning her.

Event: Fathers many children, including out of wedlock
Explanation: He spreads his genes but causes scandal, disownment, and eventual downfall for himself and turmoil for others.

Event: Liberates Ziyal from a Breen work camp with Kira
Explanation: He risks himself to free his daughter, securing her survival and their bond.

Event: Drops off the map and becomes an archaeologist of the Pah-wraiths
Explanation: He gains knowledge and a new path but this study ultimately drags him toward destructive fanaticism.

Event: Has Bajorans join Dominion and becomes Cardassia’s head of state
Explanation: He maximizes his personal authority at the cost of massive future casualties and instability.

Event: Aligns himself with the Pah-wraiths as their Emissary
Explanation: He gains occult power but ends in eternal entombment while pushing a genocidal agenda.

Event: Fires at hallucinations and beats injured Sisko with a pipe
Explanation: He vents his rage, worsens his mental state, and fails to achieve lasting advantage.

Event: Allows Pah-wraith possession to gain power
Explanation: He feels loved and empowered briefly but this seals his fate as their doomed instrument.

Event: Promises Sisko he’ll be “watching” in case Starfleet needs help
Explanation: He cultivates influence while occasionally providing real assistance, even if self-serving.

Event: Saves Sisko from destroyed USS Honshu to later demand respect then kill him
Explanation: He preserves his foe for a theatrical confrontation that fails and exposes his instability.

Event: Reluctantly gives up position as Prefect and evacuates station
Explanation: He loses power but his withdrawal allows Bajor a chance at recovery and autonomy.

Event: Restyles himself as a rebel and mounts guerrilla war on Klingons
Explanation: He regains purpose and leverage while inflicting real damage on a hostile occupier.

Event: Sells out Cardassia to the Dominion for power
Explanation: He secures personal rule in exchange for his people becoming subservient cannon fodder.

Event: Vows not to let Dukat destroy Bajor and resolve “him or me”
Explanation: He commits to eliminating an existential threat, ultimately preventing galactic-scale catastrophe.

Event: Targets disabled enemy ships’ bridges to kill everyone aboard
Explanation: He improves tactical odds via lethal efficiency at needless cost to opponents’ lives.

Event: Destroys the Groumall with stranded Klingon crew aboard
Explanation: He preempts humiliation for them by annihilating them, gaining only minor security benefit.

Event: Destroys another Klingon Bird-of-Prey instead of trying deception
Explanation: He chooses guaranteed slaughter over a possible bloodless solution, gaining only safety from exposure.

Event: Runs con to seduce Bajoran women (fake harassment, then “rescuer”)
Explanation: He gains sex and ego strokes via staged abuse that manipulates and exploits victims.

Event: Successfully corrupts Kai Winn toward the Pah-wraiths
Explanation: He advances his cult but helps create a volatile partner who later betrays him and dies.

Event: Implements sweeping reforms to improve Bajoran camp conditions
Explanation: He modestly enhances his image while significantly reducing death and suffering in the camps.

Event: Publicly acknowledges Ziyal as his daughter
Explanation: He sacrifices career and status to give her identity and family connection.

Event: Conducts one-ship guerrilla campaign against Klingons using captured Bird-of-Prey
Explanation: He boosts his prestige while dealing real blows to an invading enemy.

Event: Advises eradicating Earth’s population be rejected to force submission
Explanation: He wants living subjects to worship him instead of dead enemies, still accepting mass oppression.

Event: Fires disruptors to destroy Xepolite ship after threats
Explanation: He opts for lethal resolution rather than testing nonviolent alternatives, gaining control of wreckage.

Event: Lets Maquis escape rather than insist on killing them
Explanation: He preserves lives and avoids escalation while accepting some tactical loss.

Event: Captains Cardassian resistance freighter after demotion
Explanation: He rebuilds relevance and inflicts costs on Klingons despite prior disgrace.

Event: Blames Bajorans and Central Command instead of accepting responsibility
Explanation: He protects his ego and some reputation while deepening others’ resentment and future harm.

Event: Kidnaps and has Rugal Pa’Dar adopted by Bajorans to hurt his rival
Explanation: He gains political leverage at the expense of a child’s life and his rival’s reputation.

Event: Tries to kill Ziyal on Ravinok to protect career
Explanation: He nearly murders his own daughter for image, risking psychological collapse and losing her if he succeeds.

Event: Spies Ziyal’s friendship with Garak and feels “betrayed” by Kira
Explanation: His possessive reaction strains relationships and fuels further obsession without benefit.

Event: Orders Garak imprisoned indefinitely in Dominion prison
Explanation: He indulges a vendetta that removes a skilled opponent but at cost of making a dangerous enemy on escape.

Event: Has Pah-wraith cult commit to mass suicide while planning to fake his own death
Explanation: He intends to erase witnesses and scandal but is exposed, loses his flock, and flees in disgrace.

Event: Sets up cult colony devoted to Pah-wraiths on Empok Nor
Explanation: He gains adoration and control briefly while endangering followers in a doomed, collapsing setup.

Event: Supplies arms to Bajoran extremist group with ties to Central Command
Explanation: He gains leverage in Bajoran politics while fueling violence and instability.

Event: Admits in “Waltz” that he should have killed all Bajorans and vows genocide
Explanation: He embraces an extermination goal that ensures total opposition and seals his eventual ruin.

Event: Tries to murder Mika via airlock to hide affair
Explanation: He risks exposure and loss of his cult for a short-term coverup already at risk of failing.

Event: Tricks followers into believing in suicide pact to cover mistress pregnancy
Explanation: He attempts to erase evidence at catastrophic cost to others, and the plan collapses completely.

Event: Aligns Cardassia with Dominion and announces “new era” speech
Explanation: He gains dictatorial power while chaining his world to an exploitative empire.

Event: Runs one-ship war rather than accept civilian government diplomacy
Explanation: He salvages personal pride and some tactical gains while undermining his own side’s policy.

Event: Attempts to seduce Kira repeatedly despite her revulsion
Explanation: He indulges his obsession and ego at ongoing psychological cost to her, gaining no reciprocation.

Event: Reveals to Kira that he kept her mother as his mistress as a power move
Explanation: He tries to destabilize her and assert dominance but only hardens her hatred and opposition.

Event: Beats and drags Sakonna when Maquis try to kidnap him
Explanation: He ensures his own survival while inflicting gratuitous violence on an opponent.

Event: Agrees to work with Sisko to expose Central Command’s DMZ arms smuggling
Explanation: He settles scores and regains influence while preventing war escalation.

Event: Helps DS9 crew on multiple occasions while nursing resentment
Explanation: He secures leverage and goodwill opportunities while their missions often genuinely benefit.

Event: Destroys disabled Klingon ships instead of leaving survivors
Explanation: He insists on total enemy annihilation, gaining only marginal security over captured POWs.

Event: Disowns Ziyal after she chooses to stay on DS9
Explanation: He sacrifices his primary emotional anchor out of wounded pride, worsening later breakdown.

Event: Attacks Sisko with pipe after seeing distress call as “betrayal”
Explanation: He vents rage, sabotages any chance of reconciliation, and fails to kill his enemy.

Event: Orders purges of civilian and military dissidents during his dictatorship
Explanation: He consolidates rule at cost of lives, further delegitimizing his regime.

Event: Helps motivate Damar to “be the leader Cardassia needs”
Explanation: He boosts his former adjutant’s resolve, indirectly enabling the later anti-Dominion resistance.

Event: Accepts being Dominion’s frontman rather than resisting their control
Explanation: He gets to play strongman while helping entrench a brutal occupation over many worlds.

Event: Attempts to re-create occupation dynamic via Pah-wraith cult on Empok Nor
Explanation: He briefly feeds his fantasy while creating another fiasco that ends his local influence.

Event: Murders Solbor when his Anjohl disguise is exposed
Explanation: He silences a threat to his cover at the cost of killing a respected cleric, deepening eventual backlash.

Event: Poisons Dukat to gain Pah-wraith power
Explanation: She attempts to remove a rival for spiritual power but instead clears the way for his resurrection and empowerment.

Event: Reanimates Dukat and grants him supernatural powers
Explanation: They empower their champion briefly but ensure a direct confrontation that leads to their defeat and entombment.

Event: Tackles Dukat into Fire Caves chasm, destroying book and trapping him
Explanation: He sacrifices his own corporeal existence to neutralize Dukat permanently and save Bajor.

Event: Reveals fake cyanide pill plan, stopping cult mass suicide
Explanation: She preserves her own integrity and prevents dozens of needless deaths.
